text: Human resources are the backbone of any organisation and are the driving force behind the realisation of organisational objectives. Although contemporary organisations use highly technical and highly automated computer technology and data banks, these materials are not used to their full potential by the staff who are entrusted with their operation. Therefore, it is the staff that decides the potential of the physical and information materials at their disposal. Human Resource Management (HRM) deals with the planning, organisation, direction and control of human resources. It has to do with the acquisition, development, motivation and compensation of human resources as well as their induction, maintenance and dismissal. One of the essential elements of the Human Resource Management function is job analysis. A job analysis is a detailed investigation of the work behaviour required for performance in a given job. It looks into the skills, knowledge and physical, psychological and social characteristics needed to perform the tasks assigned to the job. It is the job analysis that links the human capacity required to the organisational objectives and secures the required skills for the achievement of these objectives and realisation of the desired development of the organisation.
